acceptance_criteria: Create a minimal, dependency-ordered M3 task DAG under project/tasks/active with explicit owner_role, reviewer_role, risk_level, dependencies, allowed_paths, forbidden_paths, acceptance_criteria, required_tests, route_to, and notes for every M3 task.

The M3 DAG covers Roadmap M3 goals without implementing them: polity, suzerain/vassal chains, obligations, administrative burden, 50-80 character fixture, relationships, offices, appointments, enfeoffment/policy, tribute and troop obligation fulfillment, postwar arrangement prototype with at least three governance methods and 24-month comparison, minimal succession state machine, appointment/bulk/reason UI, H-003/H-005 initial validation, historical claim pipeline, and M3 exit validation.
Tasks are small enough for independent writer/reviewer routing and do not create a single giant M3 implementation task.
No product code, package code, content-source data, docs/roadmap edits, frozen ADR changes, or M3 implementation work is performed by this task.
systems_architect writer and independent qa_reviewer reviewer produce validated handoffs before lead acceptance.
required_tests: node -e JSON validation for all new M3 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency existence/status shape
notes: M3 is polity, office, character, and vassal vertical-slice planning. Do not implement M3 functionality in this task.
M3 task graph must preserve frozen decisions in PROJECT_STATUS.md and M5 Human Gate policy.
Historical people, titles, claims, and cultural content must remain claim-labeled and reviewable; this graph may schedule a claim pipeline but must not create formal historical content.
Created a dependency-ordered M3 DAG with downstream implementation and validation tasks left in DRAFT for lead selection after QA review.

Downstream tasks are split across historical claim pipeline, simulation/protocol/save work, client read-model/UI work, validation, and M3 exit review; no M3 implementation is performed by this planning task.
No product, package, content, roadmap, architecture, or implementation files were changed.
